steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Apply the triangle angle sum theorem
steps.content: In any triangle, the sum of the three interior angles equals 180°. We have angle A = 47°, angle B = 90°, and angle C = (k/2)°. Setting up the equation: $$47 + 90 + \frac{k}{2} = 180$$

steps.title: Solve for k
steps.content: Simplify the left side: $$137 + \frac{k}{2} = 180$$ Subtract 137 from both sides: $$\frac{k}{2} = 43$$ Multiply both sides by 2: $$k = 86$$ That is the correct answer.
